content: The value of integral $$\int \frac{d x}{(1+x)^{3 / 4}(x-2)^{5 / 4}}$$ is is equal to
explanation:
$$\begin{aligned} \text { Let } I & =\int \frac{d x}{(x+1)^{3 / 4}(x-2)^{5 / 4}} \\ & =\int \frac{d x}{\left(\frac{x+1}{x-2}\right)^{3 / 4}(x-2)^2} \end{aligned}$$

On putting, $$\frac{x+1}{x-2}=t \Rightarrow \frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{x+1}{x-2}\right)=\frac{d t}{d x}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{(x-2) \frac{d}{d x}(x+1)-(x+1) \frac{d}{d x}(x-2)}{(x-2)^2}=\frac{d t}{d x} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{(x-2)(1)-(x+1)(1)}{(x-2)^2}=\frac{d t}{d x} \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad \frac{-3}{(x-2)^2} d x=d t \\ & \text { So, } I=\int \frac{d t}{-3 t^{3 / 4}}=-\frac{1}{3}\left(\frac{t^{-3 / 4+1}}{-\frac{3}{4}+1}\right)+C \\ & =-\frac{1}{3}\left(4 t^{1 / 4}\right)+C=-\frac{4}{3}\left(\frac{x+1}{x-2}\right)^{1 / 4}+C \\ & \end{aligned}$$

content: Let $$f(x)=\int \frac{\sqrt{x}}{(1+x)^2} d x$$, where $$x \geq 0$$. Then, $$f(3)-f(1)$$ is equal to
explanation:
$$\text { We have, } f(x)=\int \frac{\sqrt{x}}{(1+x)^2} d x \text {, where } x \geq 0$$

$$\text { Let } I=\int \frac{\sqrt{x}}{(1+x)^2} d x$$

On putting, $$x=\tan ^2 \theta \Rightarrow d x=2 \tan \theta \sec ^2 \theta d \theta$$

$$\begin{aligned} \therefore \quad & =\int \frac{(\tan \theta)\left(2 \tan \theta \sec ^2 \theta\right)}{\left(1+\tan ^2 \theta\right)^2} d \theta \\ & =\int \frac{2 \tan ^2 \theta \sec ^2 \theta}{\sec ^4 \theta} d \theta \\ & =2 \int \sin ^2 \theta d \theta \\ & =2 \int \frac{1-\cos 2 \theta}{2} d \theta \\ & =\theta-\frac{\sin 2 \theta}{2}+C \\ & =\theta-\frac{\tan \theta}{1+\tan ^2 \theta}+C=\tan ^{-1} \sqrt{x}-\frac{\sqrt{x}}{1+x}+C \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} & \therefore f(3)-f(1)=\left(\tan ^{-1} \sqrt{3}-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{1+3}+C\right) \\ & -\left(\tan ^{-1} \sqrt{1}-\frac{\sqrt{1}}{1+1}+C\right) \\ & =\frac{\pi}{3}-\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{1}{2}-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}=\frac{\pi}{12}+\frac{1}{2}-\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} \end{aligned} $$

content: Let $$f(x) = \int {{{{x^2}dx} \over {(1 + {x^2})(1 + \sqrt {1 + {x^2}} )}}} $$ and $$f(0) = 0$$, then the value of $$f(1)$$ be
explanation:
$$f(x) = \int {{{{x^2}dx} \over {(1 + {x^2})(1 + \sqrt {1 + {x^2}} )}}} $$

Let $$x = \tan \theta $$

$$ \Rightarrow dx = {\sec ^2}\theta d\theta = (1 + {x^2})d\theta $$

$$f(x) = \int {{{{x^2}dx} \over {(1 + {x^2})(1 + \sqrt {1 + {x^2}} )}}} $$

$$ = \int {{{{{\tan }^2}\theta {{\sec }^2}\theta d\theta } \over {{{\sec }^2}\theta (1 + \sec \theta )}} = \int {{{{{\tan }^2}\theta d\theta } \over {1 + \sec \theta }}} } $$

$$ = \int {{{{{\sin }^2}\theta d\theta } \over {\cos \theta (1 + \cos \theta )}}} $$

$$ = \int {{{1 - {{\cos }^2}\theta d\theta } \over {\cos \theta (1 + \cos \theta )}}} $$

$$ = \int {{{(1 - \cos \theta )d\theta } \over {\cos \theta }}} $$

$$ = \int {\sec \theta d\theta - \int {d\theta } } $$

$$ = \log (x + \sqrt {1 + {x^2}} ) - {\tan ^{ - 1}}x + C$$

$$\therefore$$ $$f(0) = \log (0 + \sqrt {1 + 0} ) - {\tan ^{ - 1}}(0) + C$$

$$0 = \log 1 - 0 + C$$

$$ \Rightarrow C = 0$$

$$\therefore$$ $$f(1) = \log (1 + \sqrt {1 + {1^2}} ) - {\tan ^{ - 1}}(1)$$

$$ = \log (1 + \sqrt 2 ) - {\pi \over 4}$$

Let $$I = \int {{1 \over {1 - 2\sin x}}dx} $$

On putting $$\sin x = {{2\tan {x \over 2}} \over {1 + {{\tan }^2}{x \over 2}}}$$, we get

$$I = \int {{1 \over {1 - {{4\tan {x \over 2}} \over {1 + {{\tan }^2}{x \over 2}}}}}dx = \int {{{1 + {{\tan }^2}{x \over 2}} \over {1 + {{\tan }^2}{x \over 2} - 4\tan {x \over 2}}}dx} } $$

$$ = \int {{{{{\sec }^2}{x \over 2}} \over {1 + {{\tan }^2}{x \over 2} - 4\tan {x \over 2}}}dx} $$

$$ = 2\int {{1 \over {{{\left( {\tan {x \over 2} - 2} \right)}^2} - {{(\sqrt 3 )}^2}}}d\left( {\tan {x \over 2}} \right)} $$

$$ = 2\int {{{dt} \over {{{(t - 2)}^2} - {{(\sqrt 3 )}^2}}}} $$, where $$t = \tan {x \over 2}$$

$$ = 2\,.\,{1 \over {2\sqrt 3 }}\log \left| {{{t - 2 - \sqrt 3 } \over {t - 2 + \sqrt 3 }}} \right| + c = {1 \over {\sqrt 3 }}\log \left| {{{\tan {x \over 2} - 2 - \sqrt 3 } \over {\tan {x \over 2} - 2 + \sqrt 3 }}} \right| + c$$
